package_name: Shutter 调参后参数按钮无反应修复 + 最小性能体检
device_feedback: Shutter horizontal adjustment changes the live preview, so runtime shutter write is effective.
After Shutter adjustment, tapping EV / WB / TINT / ISO / S appears unresponsive or blocked.

confirmed_by_code: Shutter writes are heavier than other parameters because they configure AVCaptureDevice exposure.
The horizontal ruler and its parent panel previously relied mainly on DragGesture.onEnded to release drag state.
If SwiftUI rebuilds the active ruler during Shutter runtime callbacks, stale dragging state could remain in the parent panel.
not_overclaimed: No physical device run was completed in this package, so the exact true-device runtime timing remains to be verified.
No evidence required a full overlay zIndex rewrite or runtime architecture rewrite.

code_cleanup.reason: This re-execution package prioritized the Shutter stall fix and avoided broad cleanup beyond the directly related drag state safeguards.

xcodebuild.command: xcodebuild -project /Users/sungning/Projects/SellerCamera/SellerCamera.xcodeproj -scheme SellerCamera -configuration Debug -destination "generic/platform=iOS Simulator" CODE_SIGNING_ALLOWED=NO build

device_run.reason: No physical device execution was performed during report creation.

known_risks: True-device validation is still required to confirm that Shutter adjustment no longer leaves the parameter console unresponsive.
If stalls persist, the next narrow investigation should inspect actual Shutter write frequency and AVCaptureDevice configuration latency on device.
This package intentionally did not perform broad CaptureScreen or runtime refactoring.
